_note: MOCK procedure result in the style of synthetic-gi-mock-v1. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD)
procedure.indication: Persistent GERD despite daily PPI (omeprazole 20 mg)
procedure.sedation: Moderate (conscious) sedation — midazolam 3 mg IV, fentanyl 100 mcg IV. Propofol avoided pending/per Anesthesiology adjudication of unconfirmed 2021 reaction; Anesthesiology reviewed the 2021 pre-anesthesia record and recommended a non-propofol sedation plan for this exam. Mild intermittent asthma noted; no periprocedural bronchospasm, SpO2 maintained 96-99% on 2 L NC.
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper endoscopy) — NPO >8 hours, adequate; clear gastric field
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum (D2) intubated; complete diagnostic exam of esophagus, stomach, and duodenum

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with biopsies. INDICATION: persistent GERD despite omeprazole 20 mg daily. SEDATION: moderate (midazolam/fentanyl); propofol avoided per Anesthesiology adjudication of unconfirmed 2021 reaction. FINDINGS: The endoscope was passed under direct vision into the esophagus. The gastroesophageal junction was located at 40 cm; a small (approximately 2 cm) sliding hiatal hernia was present. In the distal esophagus there were several linear mucosal breaks, some confluent between the tops of adjacent folds — consistent with LA Grade B erosive esophagitis. No circumferential ulceration, no stricture, and no obvious salmon-colored columnar mucosa/tongue suggestive of Barrett's; no mass or luminal narrowing. The stomach was examined in forward and retroflexed views: mild antral erythema consistent with gastritis, no ulcer, no mass; retained mucus cleared without difficulty. Gastric antral and body biopsies obtained for Helicobacter pylori. The pylorus was normal and traversed; the duodenal bulb and second portion were normal without ulcer or mass; duodenal biopsies obtained to evaluate for celiac disease given the biopsy-during-exam protocol. Distal esophageal biopsies obtained to exclude eosinophilic esophagitis and to characterize inflammation. No active bleeding at any point. IMPRESSION: (1) LA Grade B erosive esophagitis. (2) Small sliding hiatal hernia. (3) Mild gastritis; H. pylori biopsies obtained. Pathology pending.

findings.finding: LA Grade B erosive esophagitis (linear mucosal breaks, some confluent)
findings.action_taken: biopsies obtained (rule out eosinophilic esophagitis / characterize inflammation)

findings.finding: Mild gastritis (antral erythema), no ulcer or mass
Normal duodenal bulb and second portion, no ulcer/mass
No Barrett's esophagus, no stricture, no mass, no active bleeding

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Tolerated non-propofol moderate sedation well; no respiratory events. GERD/erosive esophagitis instructions reviewed; return precautions given.

followup.recommendation: Step up acid suppression for LA Grade B erosive esophagitis — increase omeprazole to 40 mg daily (or twice daily) and plan repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm mucosal healing and exclude underlying Barrett's masked by inflammation
Review H. pylori biopsy result; treat with eradication therapy if positive and confirm cure with post-treatment testing
Review esophageal and duodenal biopsy pathology (eosinophilic esophagitis / celiac); communicate results to patient
Ensure the Anesthesiology propofol adjudication outcome is documented durably in the allergy record so future proceduralists have the corrected assessment

planted_followup_items.description: H. pylori biopsy (gastric antrum/body) pending — a positive result requires eradication therapy and confirmatory cure testing
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review result; if positive start eradication therapy and schedule test-of-cure

planted_followup_items.description: Distal esophageal biopsies pending (eosinophilic esophagitis vs reflux esophagitis) and duodenal biopsies pending (celiac disease)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review pathology and communicate results to patient

planted_followup_items.description: LA Grade B erosive esophagitis warrants repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks after PPI step-up to confirm healing and exclude Barrett's esophagus obscured by active inflammation
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule follow-up EGD after acid-suppression optimization

planted_followup_items.description: Acid suppression step-up: increase omeprazole from 20 mg to 40 mg daily (or BID) for erosive esophagitis; ensure order placed and patient instructed. (No anticoagulant/antiplatelet was held — this is a PPI dose titration, not a medication resumption.)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place updated PPI order and counsel patient

planted_followup_items.description: Anesthesiology propofol allergy adjudication outcome (2021 reaction was unconfirmed urticaria, not anaphylaxis) must be reconciled into the durable AllergyIntolerance record to guide future sedation
planted_followup_items.expected_action: update allergy record with adjudicated sedation plan; close out the discrepancy

planted_followup_items.description: If repeat EGD confirms healed mucosa with no Barrett's, place on symptom-driven follow-up; if Barrett's is found on repeat exam, enter appropriate surveillance program
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set surveillance interval based on repeat EGD findings

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. SYNTHETIC data.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I'm burping up a lot of air since the scope. Nothing terrible.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat plus insufflation-related bloating/belching
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after upper endoscopy and self-limited within 24-48h. Return precautions if fever, chest pain, or trouble swallowing develop.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: Since last night I've had a fever and this deep chest and upper back pain, and it really hurts to swallow — worse than a sore throat. I feel pretty lousy.
contacts.clinical_signal: Fever + chest/back pain + odynophagia 1-2 days after esophageal biopsy — concerning for esophageal perforation / deep mucosal injury with early mediastinitis
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate evaluation / ED referral; NPO; urgent clinician contact and imaging (CT chest / contrast esophagram) to exclude esophageal perforation. Do not manage as simple sore throat.
contacts.rationale: Fever with chest/back pain and odynophagia after esophageal instrumentation is a red-flag presentation for perforation/mediastinitis and requires urgent action — this is the one true complication in this timeline.

contacts.verbatim: When will my biopsy results be back, and should I already be taking the higher dose of the omeprazole they mentioned?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and the PPI step-up instruction
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies pending (H. pylori, esophageal, duodenal), expected back ~2026-08-11; team will communicate results. Confirm omeprazole step-up to 40 mg daily per the erosive esophagitis plan and that a repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks is planned.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending pathology and the PPI titration items.
